rationale: Both land on the correct verdict (close as not-reproducible, contaminated session likely cause), but X goes further with specific file:line grounding (qwen3_tts_talker.py, deploy/qwen3_tts.yaml, stage_config.py) and offers concrete, non-obvious next-step diagnostics (PIECEWISE test, per-step EOS logit instrumentation) that add real value if the bug recurs, plus flags an adjacent determinism caveat. Y is safer and more faithful to the thread's hedged uncertainty (doesn't overclaim a mechanism) but is largely a restatement of the two thread comments with less independent investigation or actionable depth. X's confident 'stale captured graph buffers / GPU contention' narrative slightly exceeds what the maintainer actually confirmed, tempering its correctness edge, but its superior grounding and completeness give it the win.
